title: Crude Oil Prices Kept In Check By Rising Onshore Inventories
section_path: Crude Oil Prices Kept In Check By Rising Onshore Inventories
section_path_text: Crude Oil Prices Kept In Check By Rising Onshore Inventories
summary:
Main
As fragile peace has been brokered in the Middle East, we look at how rising onshore crude inventories across the globe kept crude oil prices in check. By Rohit Rathod
Over the past month, crude oil prices have been pretty volatile, driven mainly by the Israel-Iran conflict in the Middle East. While this conflict had the potential to further escalate, causing major trade flow disruptions, which would have caused oil prices to shoot past $90/bbl, they did not.
